questions.question: What is the primary website URL where you access NotebookLM?
choices: notebook.google.com
notebooklm.google.com
googlelm.notebook.com
ai.google.com/notebook
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly right—notebooklm.google.com is the correct address to sign in and access the tool.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The correct URL is notebooklm.google.com. This is the official Google AI tool address where you sign in with your Google account.

questions.question: What is the key difference between how NotebookLM works and a general AI tool with web access?
choices: NotebookLM is faster because it searches the web automatically
NotebookLM works only from sources you give it, with no open web searches or hallucinated context
NotebookLM can access any website without you needing to provide a URL
NotebookLM uses external sources to improve accuracy beyond what you provide
questions.feedback_correct: That's correct—NotebookLM is designed to work exclusively from the sources you provide, which keeps the output grounded and traceable.
questions.feedback_incorrect: NotebookLM actually does not browse the web or pull in outside context. It works only from the sources you give it, which is a feature because it means every insight can be traced back to your material.

questions.question: In what order should you perform the main steps of this exercise?
choices: Open NotebookLM, generate Video Overview, create notebook, add source
Create notebook, add source, open Studio, generate Video Overview
Open NotebookLM, create notebook, add source, open Studio, generate Video Overview
Add source, create notebook, open NotebookLM, generate Video Overview
questions.feedback_correct: Perfect—that is the exact sequence: open the tool, create your workspace, add your material, access Studio, and then generate your output.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The correct sequence is: open NotebookLM, create a new notebook, add a website source, open Studio, and then generate the Video Overview. Each step builds on the previous one.

questions.question: What does it mean when your source appears in the Sources list with a title and summary beneath it?
choices: You need to click Add Source again to complete the process
NotebookLM has finished processing the page and your material is ready to use
The source has been shared with other users in your workspace
You must wait another few minutes before you can proceed
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—that title and summary are your confirmation that NotebookLM has read, indexed, and prepared your material and you can move forward.
questions.feedback_incorrect: When the title and summary appear in the Sources panel, that is your signal that processing is complete and your material is ready to work with. You can proceed to the next step.

questions.question: Where do you access Studio within NotebookLM?
choices: In a separate browser window that opens automatically
Only through the main Settings menu
In a panel usually on the left side or along the bottom of the screen
After you generate your first Video Overview
questions.feedback_correct: That's right—Studio is accessible as a panel within your notebook interface, typically on the left side or along the bottom depending on your view.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Studio is a panel within the NotebookLM interface itself, usually found on the left side of the screen or along the bottom. You access it before generating content, not after.

questions.question: What happens when you click Generate for a Video Overview?
choices: The video is produced instantly in under one second
NotebookLM builds a structured presenter-style summary from your source material, which takes a few seconds to a couple of minutes
You are prompted to provide detailed instructions about what content to include
The tool automatically searches the web for additional context to enhance the video
questions.feedback_correct: Correct—NotebookLM builds the video from your source material, and it takes a bit of time because real work is happening. That wait is completely normal.
questions.feedback_incorrect: When you click Generate, NotebookLM takes a few seconds to a couple of minutes to create the video. It is doing real work by building a structured summary from only your source material, not from the web.

questions.question: How should you know whether the Video Overview output is trustworthy and grounded?
choices: You should assume it is always correct because it comes from an AI tool
You should verify that the structure and content draw directly from the source you added, not from outside sources
You should check multiple websites to confirm what the video says
You should trust it only if NotebookLM explicitly tells you the source of each fact
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly right—grounded AI output means you can trace everything back to your source material, so reviewing that direct connection confirms the output is trustworthy.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The way to verify the output is grounded is to notice how closely the content tracks to the source you provided. The topics, framing, and details should all come from your material, not from external sources.

questions.question: According to the course, what is the most practical next step after completing this exercise?
choices: Share your Video Overview with your entire organization immediately
Generate Audio Overviews instead because they are more advanced
Open a new notebook this week with something from your actual work and run the same steps
Wait several weeks before using NotebookLM again to ensure you have fully learned the tool
questions.feedback_correct: That is exactly right—applying what you learned to real material from your own work this week is where the tool becomes genuinely useful to you.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The best next step is to apply the skill you just practiced to your actual work this week—add a real document, report, or webpage you need to work with and run the same steps.

questions.question: How does the grounded nature of NotebookLM affect what kinds of sources work best with the tool?
choices: Only very long documents work well because they provide more context
The tool works best with your own material—reports, articles, PDFs, documents—that you want to synthesize or understand quickly
News articles work best because they are the most reliable source type
External websites are strongly preferred over personal documents or notes
questions.feedback_correct: Perfect—NotebookLM is most useful when you bring it your own material, whether that is reports, articles, notes, or other documents you actually need to work with.
questions.feedback_incorrect: NotebookLM is designed to work with your actual material—PDFs, Google Docs, notes, articles, or any content you genuinely need to understand or process. That is where it earns its value.
